name: dprint
description: A pluggable and configurable code formatting platform written in Rust

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Initializes a configuration file in the current directory

subcommands.name: fmt
subcommands.description: Formats the source files and writes the result to the file system

name: --diff
options.description: Outputs a check-like diff of every formatted file

name: --stdin
options.description: Format stdin and output the result to stdout. Provide an absolute file path to apply the inclusion and exclusion rules or an extension or file name to always format the text

name: --incremental
options.description: Only format files when they change. This may alternatively be specified in the configuration file

name: --excludes
options.description: List of file patterns or directories in quotes to exclude when formatting. This overrides what is specified in the config file

name: --allow-node-modules
options.description: Allows traversing node module directories (unstable - This flag will be renamed to be non-node specific in the future)

args.name: files
args.description: List of file patterns in quotes to format. This overrides what is specified in the config file

subcommands.name: check
subcommands.description: Checks for any files that haven't been formatted

name: --incremental
options.description: Only format files when they change. This may alternatively be specified in the configuration file

name: --excludes
options.description: List of file patterns or directories in quotes to exclude when formatting. This overrides what is specified in the config file

name: --allow-node-modules
options.description: Allows traversing node module directories (unstable - This flag will be renamed to be non-node specific in the future)

args.name: files
args.description: List of file patterns in quotes to format. This overrides what is specified in the config file

subcommands.name: config
subcommands.description: Functionality related to the configuration file

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Adds a plugin to the configuration file

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Initializes a configuration file in the current directory

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Updates the plugins in the configuration file

name: -y
--yes
options.description: Upgrade process plugins without prompting to confirm checksums

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: Prints the help of the given subcommand(s)

args.name: SUBCOMMAND
args.description: The subcommand whose help message to display

subcommands.name: output-file-paths
subcommands.description: Prints the resolved file paths for the plugins based on the args and configuration

name: --excludes
options.description: List of file patterns or directories in quotes to exclude when formatting. This overrides what is specified in the config file

name: --allow-node-modules
options.description: Allows traversing node module directories (unstable - This flag will be renamed to be non-node specific in the future)

args.name: files
args.description: List of file patterns in quotes to format. This overrides what is specified in the config file

subcommands.name: output-resolved-config
subcommands.description: Prints the resolved configuration for the plugins based on the args and configuration

subcommands.name: output-format-times
subcommands.description: Prints the amount of time it takes to format each file. Use this for debugging

name: --incremental
options.description: Only format files when they change. This may alternatively be specified in the configuration file

name: --excludes
options.description: List of file patterns or directories in quotes to exclude when formatting. This overrides what is specified in the config file

name: --allow-node-modules
options.description: Allows traversing node module directories (unstable - This flag will be renamed to be non-node specific in the future)

args.name: files
args.description: List of file patterns in quotes to format. This overrides what is specified in the config file

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: Prints the help of the given subcommand(s)

args.name: SUBCOMMAND
args.description: The subcommand whose help message to display

name: -c
--config
options.description: Path or url to JSON configuration file. Defaults to dprint.json or .dprint.json in current or ancestor directory when not provided

name: --plugins
options.description: List of urls or file paths of plugins to use. This overrides what is specified in the config file
